What is the worst-case lookup time? What is the worst-case space usage?
A hash table is a data structure that uses a hash function to map keys to indices in an array. The hash function takes a key as input and returns an index in the array. The array is called the hash table. The hash function is designed to distribute the keys evenly across the array, so that the lookup time is O(1) on average. The worst-case lookup time is O(n), where n is the number of keys in the hash table. The worst-case space usage is O(n), where n is the number of keys in the hash table.
A hash table is a data structure that uses a hash function to map keys to indices in an array
